TSLA: Cramer: Tesla can’t trade on hopes forever. Jim Cramer emphasizes that Tesla's stock can't rely on future hopes indefinitely, as 2025 financial results showed significant declines in revenue, operating income, and vehicle deliveries. While there's a theoretical bull case based on robotaxis, Optimus robots, and energy business growth, the market is currently valuing Tesla like a tech platform rather than a car company, with high P/E multiples. Cramer suggests investors need tangible proof of these future ventures or else the stock risks reverting to traditional auto industry valuations, but still cautions against betting against Elon Musk's long-term vision.

Tesla (TSLA) closes above ___ on April 8? Trading Odds & Predictions. This Polymarket article outlines the trading odds and predictions for Tesla (TSLA) closing prices on April 8, 2026, featuring various price targets and their associated probabilities. It details the resolution rules for the prediction market, based on Yahoo Finance closing prices, including provisions for shortened sessions, trading halts, and corporate actions like stock splits. The article also provides a retrospective on Tesla's stock performance on April 8, noting its closing price of $343.25 amidst volatility due to Q1 2026 delivery shortfalls and future strategic moves into robotaxi and robotics.

Travis County votes to withhold some Tesla incentives. Travis County has decided to withhold some incentives from Tesla, despite the company significantly exceeding its investment and job creation targets for its gigafactory. The county cited insufficient reporting from Tesla as the reason for cutting funds. This decision comes despite Tesla's massive overperformance on its agreed-upon goals.

Intel joins Elon Musk’s Terafab chip manufacturing initiative. Intel Corp. announced its participation in Elon Musk's Terafab initiative, a collaboration between SpaceX and Tesla focused on building a semiconductor manufacturing hub for AI and robotics chips. Intel's involvement, which caused its shares to climb 4%, could include plant construction, maintenance, and providing its advanced chip packaging technologies. The Terafab campus in Texas aims to produce 1 TW/year of compute and will feature two fabs for edge processors and orbital AI data center chips.

10 Consumer Discretionary Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session. This article details "whale activity" in ten Consumer Discretionary sector stocks, including Amazon.com, Tesla, and Carnival, based on unusual options trading. It provides a table and bullet-by-bullet explanations of specific call and put option sweeps and trades, indicating sentiment (bullish, bearish, neutral), expiration dates, strike prices, total trade prices, open interest, and volume for each company. The author emphasizes that such alerts can help traders identify potential significant trading opportunities, as large-sum transactions can cause option prices to deviate from their normal values.
TGT: Shoppers warned over long lines as Target self-checkout lanes are CUT at stores. Rhode Island lawmakers are considering a bill that would limit self-checkout kiosks in stores like Target and Walmart to a maximum of eight per store. The proposal also requires one cashier for every two self-checkout machines and additional staff to oversee them, potentially leading to longer lines for shoppers. Supporters argue it protects jobs and ensures grocery workers' hours, while retailers claim self-checkout helps manage small orders and improve efficiency.

Target outperformance signals shift to more durable growth, says Jefferies. Jefferies has reiterated a 'Buy' rating on Target (NYSE:TGT) with a $140 price target, noting the company's shares are up 20% this year due to improving business trends. Analysts highlighted stabilized margins, increasing contributions from higher-margin revenue streams like advertising and membership, and effective cost control under CEO Michael Fiddelke as key drivers. The firm believes Target's outperformance reflects a shift towards more durable growth, anticipating continued momentum and earnings growth through operational improvements.

Target brings back Car Seat Trade-In program for 10th year. Target is bringing back its Car Seat Trade-In program for the 10th year, offering shoppers a 20% Target Circle Bonus for trading in old car seats. This year, the bonus can be applied not just to new car seats or baby gear, but also to toys, food and beverage, and apparel. The program has recycled over 58 million pounds of materials, preventing them from ending up in landfills.

Popular discount retailer opening new store in Henrietta. Ross Dress for Less is expanding its presence in the Rochester, NY area with a new store opening in Henrietta, next to Target, and another underway in Irondequoit. The Henrietta location involves a $1.2 million renovation of a former Babies R Us, while the Irondequoit store takes over a former Big Lots space. These additions will bring the total number of Ross stores in the greater Rochester market to four.

Target Corp. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors. Target Corp. (TGT) stock declined by 2.20% on Tuesday, closing at $119.52. This performance was in contrast to the broader stock market, as the S&P 500 Index rose, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell slightly. The company's stock finished 5.14% below its 52-week high.
SBUX: Marcus & Millichap Arranges Sale, Financing of Starbucks Property in Joliet. Marcus & Millichap has successfully arranged the sale and financing of a net-leased Starbucks property in Joliet, Illinois, for $2.9 million. The property, built in 2025 and secured by a 15-year ground lease, was sold to an out-of-state private investor at 97% of its list price. The transaction was facilitated by Marcus & Millichap's teams in Chicago Oak Brook and Milwaukee, with financing secured at a 5.65% fixed interest rate over five years.

Pumpkin Spice Latte: Starbucks' Iconic Fall Beverage. Starbucks' Pumpkin Spice Latte (PSL), introduced in 2003, is an iconic seasonal beverage blending espresso, pumpkin spice flavors, steamed milk, and whipped cream, symbolizing the start of fall for many consumers. It generates massive demand and billions in sales annually, with its cultural relevance amplified by social media. The article details its unique ingredients, consumer appeal, global market impact, and the sustained innovation and marketing efforts that maintain its dominance in a competitive landscape.

Starbucks Quietly Launches New Spring Drinks—Including a Caffeinated “Energy Refresher” Line. Starbucks has launched nine new drinks for a late spring release, featuring a new "Energy Refresher" line with approximately 75 mg of extra caffeine and a "Mango Cream Cold Foam." The new offerings include various mango-strawberry refresher options, new matcha and chai drinks with mango cream, and an Iced Ube Coconut Cream Shaken Espresso, following the success of a previous ube macchiato.
DIS: The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) Stock Signal: Will Streaming Growth and Insider Buying Lift Shares?. This article provides a bullish analysis of The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS), highlighting its streaming segment's expected growth and recent insider buying as key drivers. It suggests a long trade idea for DIS with specific entry, take profit, and stop loss levels, supported by fundamental and technical analysis. The author, Adam Lemon, remains optimistic due to strategic shifts in monetization for streaming, attractive valuations, and encouraging theme park developments.

The Walt Disney Company breaks a News & Documentary Emmy record with 96 nominations. The Walt Disney Company set a new record with 96 News & Documentary Emmy Award nominations for its content brands and studios, ABC News and National Geographic. National Geographic earned 51 nominations, with "Trafficked with Mariana van Zeller" receiving 25 nods, making it the most nominated single title for the third consecutive year. ABC News secured 47 nominations, its highest since 1969, including 19 for ABC News Studios and 13 for ABC News Live.

The Walt Disney Company breaks a News & Documentary Emmy record with 96 nominations. The Walt Disney Company has set a new record with 96 News & Documentary Emmy Award nominations for its content brands and studios, ABC News and National Geographic. National Geographic earned 51 nominations, with "Trafficked with Mariana van Zeller" receiving 25 nods, while ABC News secured 47 nominations, its highest total since 1969. The winners for news and documentary categories will be announced in separate ceremonies on May 27th and May 28th, respectively, in New York City.

The Walt Disney Company breaks a News & Documentary Emmy record with 96 nominations. The Walt Disney Company has set a new record by receiving 96 News & Documentary Emmy Award nominations across its content brands, including ABC News and National Geographic. National Geographic led with 51 nominations, and "Trafficked with Mariana van Zeller" was the most nominated single title for the third consecutive year. ABC News also achieved its highest number of nominations since 1969 with 47 nods, with "ABC World News Tonight with David Muir" nominated for Outstanding Live News Program for the fifth year.
NKE: CEO Gaffe of the Week: Deckers Outdoor. This article criticizes Deckers Outdoor's CEO, Angel Martinez, for what the author deems poor decision-making, specifically highlighting an ill-conceived advertising campaign featuring Tom Brady in New York City. The article also discusses Deckers' ongoing struggles with international expansion costs, rising sheepskin prices, and the company's dependency on the Ugg brand. It contrasts Deckers' performance with other companies like Michael Kors, Nike, Ford, and FedEx, which have also faced challenges in Europe.
